note: v1.2: ActivityItem cross-checked against backend/routers/activity.py — structural mismatch documented. Regenerate from Pydantic source with: cd backend && python generate_schema.py
description: API contract between SportsHub FastAPI backend and iOS Swift client. JSON field names must match iOS CodingKey string literals. Used by validate_api_contract.sh for schema drift detection.

known_mismatch_reasons.future-backed: iOS has a stub OR backend sends this; the other side will catch up in a future sprint
known_mismatch_reasons.client-only: field is derived/computed on the client; it never comes from or goes to the backend
known_mismatch_reasons.deprecated: field exists on one side but should be removed or renamed; tracked for a cleanup sprint

notes: Real multipart upload pipeline implemented in Phase 4b.
iOS FileUploadToken.swift has explicit CodingKeys for all 4 fields.
12-field response. iOS EvidenceResponse struct maps all fields via explicit CodingKeys.

Backend Pydantic schema has 9 fields. iOS struct decodes 5 additional optional fields (challenger_submitted_score, opponent_submitted_score, accepted_at, completed_at, winner_user_id) that some routers return as extra dict fields beyond the Pydantic model.
The 5 iOS-only extras decode as nil when absent — not a runtime failure.

KNOWN GAP: challenger_confirmed and opponent_confirmed are required backend fields not present in iOS ChallengeResponse. These should be added to show awaiting-confirmation UI state. Tracked as pre-existing gap.

challenger_submitted_score.note: some routers return this as an extra dict field; will be standardised into the ChallengeResponse Pydantic schema

accepted_at.note: extra dict field from some routers; not yet in Pydantic schema

completed_at.note: extra dict field from some routers; not yet in Pydantic schema

winner_user_id.note: extra dict field from some routers; not yet in Pydantic schema

challenger_confirmed.note: backend already sends this as a required field; iOS needs a CodingKey + awaiting-confirmation UI state

opponent_confirmed.note: backend already sends this as a required field; iOS needs a CodingKey + awaiting-confirmation UI state

notes: Fixed 2026-04-18: iOS startsAt/endsAt/participantCount CodingKeys corrected to match backend start_date/end_date/current_participants.

iOS Tournament struct declares many fields absent from backend schema (tournament_type, ranked_type, team_size, min_elo, max_elo, current_round, is_public, is_school, is_regional, region, school_name, prizes). These decode as nil/0/[] since backend does not send them — iOS-side future feature stubs.
Backend sends creator_username, is_premium_only, location, is_online, entry_fee, prize_description — not decoded by iOS; tournament list UI does not display these fields.

creator_username.note: backend sends; iOS will decode when creator attribution is shown in tournament detail UI

is_premium_only.note: backend sends; iOS will decode when premium-only tournament badge is added

location.note: backend sends; iOS will decode when venue/location display is added

is_online.note: backend sends; iOS will decode when online vs in-person filter is added

entry_fee.note: backend sends; iOS will decode when entry fee display and payment flow are added

prize_description.note: backend sends; iOS will decode when prize detail section is added

tournament_type.note: iOS stub (decodes as nil); backend will add this field when tournament type system ships

ranked_type.note: iOS stub; backend will add ranked vs unranked distinction

team_size.note: iOS stub; backend will add when team tournament support ships

min_elo.note: iOS stub; backend will add ELO floor gating for tournament entry

max_elo.note: iOS stub; backend will add ELO ceiling gating

current_round.note: iOS stub; backend will add bracket round tracking

is_public.note: iOS stub; backend will add public/private tournament toggle

is_school.note: iOS stub; backend will add school league / intramural support

is_regional.note: iOS stub; backend will add regional tournament designation

region.note: iOS stub; backend will add region field

school_name.note: iOS stub; backend will add school name for school leagues

prizes.note: iOS stub (array); backend will add structured prize list

notes: IMPORTANT: iOS User struct has NO CodingKeys enum. Swift Codable synthesises JSON keys from property names directly, so camelCase properties look for camelCase JSON keys. Backend sends snake_case. This means displayName looks for key 'displayName', not 'display_name'.
iOS User struct decodes 8 properties; backend UserResponse has 13 fields. Only id/email/username/role match correctly (4 of 8 iOS properties).
bio is present in UserProfile (extends UserResponse) but not in UserResponse itself — may or may not be in the /users/me response depending on endpoint return type.

account_status.note: backend sends; iOS will decode for account suspension / moderation warnings UI

age_verified.note: backend sends; iOS will decode for COPPA gate and age verification badge

email_verified.note: backend sends; iOS will decode to show email verification status badge

survey_completed.note: backend sends; iOS will decode to skip onboarding survey for returning users

is_legacy_account.note: backend sends; iOS will decode for legacy migration messaging

created_at.note: backend sends; iOS will decode for member-since display in Profile

full_name.note: backend sends; iOS will decode when real name is surfaced in profile UI

is_admin.note: backend sends; iOS admin routing currently uses user.role via SessionManager.isAdmin

dateOfBirth.note: not in backend UserResponse; stored locally for age verification display / COPPA compliance UI

parentEmail.note: not in backend UserResponse; parent account info stored locally for COPPA compliance UI

bio.note: present in UserProfile (extended schema); will be in /users/me once endpoint returns UserProfile instead of UserResponse

notes: Backend returns a raw Python dict — no Pydantic model. Fields confirmed from check_evidence_required() in evidence.py.

Backend sends a deeply-nested dict: { type, timestamp, challenge_id, sport, match_type, challenger: {id, username, display_name}, opponent: {id, username, display_name}, winner: {id, username, display_name}|null, rating_change: {challenger: int, opponent: int}|null }.
iOS ActivityItem expects flat scalar fields: user_id, username, opponent_username, winner_username, user_score, opponent_score, rating_change (Int?), created_at.
RESULT: only 'type', 'sport', and 'match_type' decode correctly. All other iOS fields are silent nil/empty. NotificationsView rows will show no usernames, no scores, no time — completely blank data.
Fix options: (A) flatten the backend dict to match iOS shape, or (B) redesign iOS ActivityItem to decode nested challenger/opponent/winner objects.

user_id.note: iOS expects flat 'user_id' string; backend sends nested challenger.id — iOS field decodes as nil. Fix: add flat user_id to backend dict or redesign iOS struct.

username.note: iOS expects flat 'username' string; backend sends nested challenger.username — decodes as nil.

opponent_username.note: iOS expects flat 'opponent_username' string; backend sends nested opponent.username — decodes as nil.

winner_username.note: iOS expects flat 'winner_username' string; backend sends nested winner.username — decodes as nil.

user_score.note: iOS expects flat 'user_score' Int?; backend does not send any score field — always nil.

opponent_score.note: iOS expects flat 'opponent_score' Int?; backend does not send any score field — always nil.

rating_change.note: iOS expects 'rating_change' as Int? (single integer); backend sends {challenger: int, opponent: int} dict or null — type mismatch, iOS decodes as nil.

created_at.note: iOS expects key 'created_at'; backend sends key 'timestamp' — decodes as nil. All notification timestamps are blank.

timestamp.note: Backend sends 'timestamp'; iOS has no CodingKey for it. Should be renamed to 'created_at' on the backend.

challenge_id.note: Backend sends challenge_id; iOS has no field for it. Useful for deep-linking into match detail from notification tap.

challenger.note: Backend sends nested challenger object {id, username, display_name}; iOS has no struct for it. Flatten or add nested decodable.

opponent.note: Backend sends nested opponent object {id, username, display_name}; iOS has no struct for it.

winner.note: Backend sends nested winner object or null; iOS has no struct for it.

timestamp.note: iOS decodes as 'created_at' — key mismatch, always nil

challenger.note: nested {id, username, display_name}; iOS has no CodingKey

opponent.note: nested {id, username, display_name}; iOS has no CodingKey

winner.note: nested {id, username, display_name} or null; iOS has no CodingKey

rating_change.note: {challenger: int, opponent: int} or null; iOS expects Int?
